title: My Full Stack Developer Journey: From Childhood Curiosity to Building Real-World Software
content: My full stack developer journey began long before I understood what programming or software development meant. My first interaction with a computer happened when I was just three or four years old, after my father bought a desktop computer for his official work. That computer unknowingly became the foundation of my lifelong curiosity with technology. The system came with several games installed, including Road Rash, Virtua Cop, DX-Ball, and Need for Speed II. I loved playing games, but what fascinated me more was the computer itself. I wanted to understand how it worked. I closely observed what my father did on the computer and tried to mimic his actions. I learned how to play audio and video files, write documents in Microsoft Word, and perform basic calculations in Excel. This early exposure helped me develop the habit of learning by exploration, even before I knew it was a skill. Around 2008, when I was about six years old, my father bought a modem and I gained internet access for the first time. It felt magical. I searched Google for whatever came to my mind and quickly learned how to download games, movies, and songs. While downloading and playing games was my favorite activity, the internet also sparked my curiosity about websites and emerging technologies. This growing passion eventually led me to pursue Computer Science and Engineering (CSE). During my academic journey, I learned my first programming language, C, followed by C++. I also explored competitive programming, practicing problem-solving on platforms like Codeforces, Beecrowd, and other online judges. These experiences strengthened my logical thinking and problem-solving mindset. Over time, I discovered a strong interest in web development. Driven by curiosity and self learning, I taught myself HTML, CSS, Tailwind CSS, JavaScript, React, Node.js, and MongoDB. Building projects and understanding how systems work end to end made web development the perfect fit for me as a developer. After graduating, I landed my first professional role, where I expanded my skill set by learning PHP and working with WordPress. Currently, I am developing a WordPress plugin called Bit CRM, gaining hands-on experience in building real world software solutions and understanding production level systems. Looking back, my journey from childhood curiosity with computers to becoming a full stack developer has been driven by exploration, consistency, and a deep interest in how technology works. What started as playing games evolved into building software, and that curiosity continues to guide my learning and growth every day.
author: Shirajul Islam Shakur
